input.headline: Why history's 'most controversial' queen was so hated

raw.headline: Why history's 'most controversial' queen was so hated

winner: Queen Elizabeth I was widely disliked because she executed her two queens—Mary and Anne. People found it a bit extreme for just one throne.

candidates.text: Why did Queen Elizabeth I get so much hate? Because she had a *reign* in with her subjects!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a play on words with 'reign' and 'scene,' which is somewhat clever, but the setup could be more specific to Elizabeth I's actual controversies. The humor is mild and relies on a common pun rather than a surprising or deeply contextual twist."}

candidates.text: Queen Elizabeth I's subjects loved her so much they wrote songs about her... like "I Like Her (But I'm Not Sure Why).
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the song reference, but it's a bit generic and doesn't fully engage with the 'most controversial' or 'hated' angle of the headline."}

candidates.text: Queen Elizabeth I once got into a heated argument with her advisors about whether or not she could actually be "both" Mary and Elizabeth—turns out the paperwork just wasn't clear.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative but lacks clarity and historical specificity; the reference to 'Mary and Elizabeth' is vague without context, reducing surprise and funniness."}

candidates.text: Queen Elizabeth I wasn't hated—she was just really good at hiding the fact that she loved her dog more than her people, and that's what made her controversial!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor falls flat due to anachronistic assumptions (e.g., Elizabeth I's relationship with her dog being a source of controversy). The punchline lacks historical accuracy and feels forced."}

candidates.text: Queen Elizabeth I was widely disliked because she executed her two queens—Mary and Anne. People found it a bit extreme for just one throne.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "The joke is confusing and historically inaccurate. Elizabeth I did not execute queens named Mary and Anne in a way that would be tied to 'just one throne.' The reference lacks specificity and clarity."}
